title: Wendy Red Star: Making Art About Her Native American Life
summary: Wendy Red Star is an artist from the Crow Indian Reservation in Montana. She uses art like photos and sculptures to show her Native American identity and share stories from her childhood. Wendy grew up riding horses and going to events like Crow Fair with her family. Her grandma made special clothes called regalia for her. In college, she learned about a chief named Sits in the Middle of the Land who helped define Crow territory. This inspired her to set up teepees on campus to honor her heritage. Wendy believes art is very important because it helps people remember who they are. She says making art is a big part of her identity.

background_read: Wendy Red Star is a Native American artist who creates art to share her culture and personal stories. She grew up on a reservation, which is land set aside for Native American communities. Art can include many things like drawing, building, or performing. Wendy's work helps others learn about her life and the history of her people, showing how art connects to identity.
Article_Structure: WHO: Wendy Red Star, a multimedia artist from the Crow Indian Reservation
WHAT: She makes art to celebrate Native identity and share childhood memories
WHY: To honor her heritage and show why art matters for remembering who we are

perspectives.description: Wendy sees art as a way to express and preserve her Native American identity and personal history.
